 {"id":"bd-5ipb","title":"ffs-fuse: validate FIEMAP ioctl request flags and honor SYNC","description":"Linux fiemap_prep validates fm_flags, rejects unsupported bits with EBADR, and performs a sync before mapping when FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC is set. ffs-fuse currently parses the FIEMAP header but ignores fm_flags entirely. Harden the FUSE ioctl path so unsupported flags are rejected and FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC triggers a pre-map fsync path before extent lookup. Add focused tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:58:52.391523869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T19:06:14.110339648Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T19:06:14.110152173Z","close_reason":"Validated FIEMAP fm_flags handling: reject unsupported bits with EBADR and fsync on FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC before extent lookup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fiemap","fuse","ioctl"]}
 {"id":"bd-5iz","title":"ffs-block: Implement ARC metadata cache (read-only first)","description":"Goal: implement an ARC-like cache wrapper for block reads to reduce random I/O during metadata-heavy operations.

Scope (v1):
- Cache read_block() results (BlockNumber -> BlockBuf).
- ARC lists: T1/T2 + ghost lists B1/B2; adaptive parameter p.
- No write-back yet; writes either bypass or invalidate.

Key design questions to answer explicitly:
- Cache key: BlockNumber only, or (BlockNumber, CommitSeq/Version) for MVCC? (If MVCC needs versioned cache, design now.)
- Concurrency: avoid holding cache locks while doing disk I/O.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover core ARC transitions (hits/misses/evictions) with deterministic sequences.
- No deadlocks; no I/O under locks.
- Benchmarks show cache improves repeated metadata reads on fixture images.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:43.959932112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:07:09.719979271Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:07:09.719959934Z","close_reason":"ARC cache: fix eviction logic + add transition tests; gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5iz","depends_on_id":"bd-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:27.296806735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-5lxot","title":"[FUSE] renameat2 RENAME_EXCHANGE atomic dual-entry swap (ext4 + btrfs)","description":"Follow-up to bd-bivg2: implement RENAME_EXCHANGE atomically in OpenFs::rename2.

Context: bd-bivg2 shipped RENAME_NOREPLACE end-to-end (FsOps::rename2 trait method, FUSE dispatcher forwards flags, OpenFs honours NOREPLACE under the parent locks). RENAME_EXCHANGE returns EINVAL until this lands.

Required work:
- ext4: rewrite both parent directory blocks atomically. If parent_x == parent_y, both edits are in one block — straightforward. If different parents, need to acquire the journal transaction and stage both block writes so a crash mid-swap reverts both. ffs-dir needs a swap_inode_in_entry(name, new_ino) primitive that reuses the existing find logic from add_entry/remove_entry.
- btrfs: dir items live in the fs-tree as DIR_ITEM keys. Swap requires updating both DIR_ITEM payloads atomically. The COW tree gives us the staging surface.
- Update timestamps + nlink: per renameat2(2) man page, only ctime is bumped on both inodes; nlink, mtime, atime are unchanged.
- Replace the EINVAL stub at OpenFs::rename2 with the real implementation.
- Replace the assertion in test ext4_rename2_exchange_returns_einval_until_implemented with a positive test that swaps two entries and verifies the post-condition lookups crossover.

Acceptance:
- Test: create x and y in same dir; renameat2(x, y, RENAME_EXCHANGE) -> lookup(x).ino == old_y_ino and lookup(y).ino == old_x_ino.
- Test: same across different parents.
- Test: stress with N=1000 random pairs from a 4-entry pool, verify the pair-swap invariant holds after every iteration.
- xfstests generic/{375, 446} pass under the FUSE mount.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:42:12.992662811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:42:12.992662811Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-5o3i6","title":"[HIGH] btrfs readdir tests fail: CRC32C checksum mismatch in fixture","description":"6 tests failing in ffs-core: btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot, btrfs_readdir_mixed_types, btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination, btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index, btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names, walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items. All panic at lib.rs:23xxx with CRC32C checksum mismatch. build_btrfs_readdir_image fixture needs tree block checksum stamping.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:04:56.247745828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:16:35.149141965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:16:35.147217765Z","close_reason":"Fixed: added CRC32C checksum stamping to build_btrfs_readdir_image fixture","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-bejk","title":"Fix dx_root test layout, read-directory EISDIR check, and close stale clippy bead","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:41.502809158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:54.140304578Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:54.139616074Z","close_reason":"Fixed 3 issues:
(1) ffs-core read() missing EISDIR check: reading a directory inode via FsOps::read() returned Ok with dir data instead of Err(EISDIR). Added is_dir() guard in ext4 read path. 471 ffs-core tests pass.
(2) ffs-ondisk parse_dx_root_basic test: dx_entry layout was wrong — test wrote explicit hash(4)+block(4) for entry 0, but dx_countlimit overlaps entry 0 (hash is implicit 0, only block at offset+4). Fixed test data layout.
(3) ffs-ondisk proptest dx_entries: same layout bug — restructured to match actual format with entry 0 block separate from subsequent hash+block pairs. 360 ffs-ondisk tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","correctness","ondisk"]}
+{"id":"bd-bivg2","title":"[FUSE] renameat2 RENAME_EXCHANGE + RENAME_NOREPLACE atomicity","description":"Wire RENAME_EXCHANGE and RENAME_NOREPLACE flags from FUSE FUSE_RENAME2 (opcode 45) through ffs-core::rename to the directory operations in ffs-dir.

Context:
- RENAME_NOREPLACE: rename fails with EEXIST if the target already exists, instead of overwriting it. Atomicity guarantee: the check + rename must be one critical section under the FuseInodeLocks parent guard so a concurrent create can't race.
- RENAME_EXCHANGE: atomically swap two existing entries (both must exist; either ENOENT). Must atomically update parent dir entries for both sides under a sorted parent-pair lock to avoid deadlock. Hash-tree (htree) directory updates need to be transactional within the journal.
- ext4 reference: fs/ext4/namei.c::ext4_rename2, ext4_rename_dir_prepare, ext4_rename_dir_finish. The exchange path swaps inode numbers in the dir entries while keeping all other fields.

Currently ffs-fuse's rename ignores the flags field on FUSE_RENAME2 — exchange degrades to dst-overwrite + src-creates loop which is non-atomic.

Acceptance:
- Test: spawn 2 threads; one does renameat2(A, B, RENAME_NOREPLACE) to existing target; the other does create(B). Either rename succeeds and create fails with EEXIST, or rename fails with EEXIST and create succeeds. Never: both succeed.
- Test: renameat2(X, Y, RENAME_EXCHANGE) where X is dir/x and Y is dir/y; verify post-condition lookup(dir, x).ino == old_y_ino && lookup(dir, y).ino == old_x_ino.
- xfstests generic/{375, 386, 446} (renameat2) pass on FUSE mount.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:27:42.013905221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:43:15.122521264Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T08:43:15.120731997Z","close_reason":"RENAME_NOREPLACE shipped end-to-end via FsOps::rename2 trait method (default impl delegates to rename for flags=0, returns EINVAL otherwise) + OpenFs override that does atomic lookup-then-rename under the parent locks the dispatcher already holds. 6 new regressions cover: classic overwrite, NOREPLACE blocks existing target with EEXIST + both names survive, NOREPLACE with free target succeeds + inode preserved, EXCHANGE returns EINVAL, NOREPLACE|EXCHANGE returns EINVAL, unknown flag returns EINVAL. RENAME_EXCHANGE atomic dual-entry swap + RENAME_WHITEOUT char-device inode work deferred to bd-5lxot.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-bx1wu","title":"[FUSE] memfd_create with MFD_HUGETLB + MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL","description":"Surface MFD_HUGETLB and MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL semantics for memfd_create(2) when the FUSE-mounted filesystem is asked to back hugepage-allocated or sealed-against-exec memory files.

Context:
- memfd_create with MFD_HUGETLB allocates the file from the hugetlbfs pool. The kernel routes this to hugetlbfs, not the FUSE backing store, so for ffs-fuse the only requirement is that we do not corrupt or refuse the call: the kernel handles it without invoking us.
- MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL (Linux 6.3+) implies MFD_ALLOW_SEALING and seals out PROT_EXEC mappings on the resulting fd. The seal lives on the inode and must persist across dup/sendmsg. For ffs-fuse, this means: F_GET_SEALS / F_ADD_SEALS via fcntl(2) must round-trip through FUSE_FALLOCATE-like ioctl plumbing or via FUSE_FCNTL once added.
- Reference: mm/memfd.c, fs/inode.c::file_seals, include/uapi/linux/memfd.h.

Most of MFD_HUGETLB does not touch the FUSE backend, but MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL needs ffs-inode to track a seal-flags bitmap on the in-memory Inode and reject mmap(PROT_EXEC) when the seal is set.

Acceptance:
- Test: memfd_create with MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL on a FUSE-mounted-backed location, then mmap with PROT_EXEC -> EACCES.
- Test: F_GET_SEALS after MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL returns F_SEAL_EXEC.
- xfstests generic/{562, 591} (memfd seal round-trip) pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:29:11.177733601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:29:11.177733601Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

This bead is to verify no other leftover debug logging remains in production code paths.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T19:06:44.798241723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T19:06:48.143728119Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T19:06:48.143438631Z","close_reason":"Already fixed by TopazHare in this session","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-c35","title":"Track: Harness + Conformance + Parity Accounting (ffs-harness)","description":"Build the proof system: fixture generation, comparisons against Linux behavior where applicable, parity reporting, and regression gates.\n\nAcceptance: every implemented behavior has (1) a fixture or property test, (2) a harness test that exercises it end-to-end, (3) FEATURE_PARITY + harness parity numbers updated in the same change. Benchmarks are added only after fixtures exist.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:52.148014837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385993623Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385971391Z","close_reason":"All 5 sub-tasks closed: ext4 fixtures (bd-2zg), parity accounting (bd-6st), fixture workflow (bd-3q4), kernel reference capture (bd-2ij), btrfs fixtures (bd-2yi).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2ij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.179975971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2yi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.099617897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.014650837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-3q4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.923718680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-6st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.825671893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-c35","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ffs-harness is the proof engine.

Without this track, progress becomes vibes instead of verified parity.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-c4vah","title":"add mounted-path btrfs hard-link refusal coverage for directory-source EPERM, non-directory parent ENOTDIR, and occupied destination EEXIST","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path FUSE E2E proves hard-link success, but not the refusal contracts that ffs-core already enforces for invalid link topologies.  {"id":"bd-ew490","title":"[HIGH] btrfs tamper-detection conformance still fails tree_block_csum validation at open","description":"Reproduced from `env AGENT_NAME=cod-fs CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22.

Failures:
- `btrfs_tree_block_checksum_tamper_detection_conforms` at `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:1103`
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` fails on the same root cause
- error: `open btrfs image: Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`

This is narrower than the umbrella conformance bead: the synthetic image used by the tamper-detection contract is still rejected before the actual tamper-detection assertion can run.

Acceptance:
- tamper-detection fixture opens successfully before the intentional corruption step
- `btrfs_tree_block_checksum_tamper_detection_conforms` passes
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this btrfs checksum slice.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:26:03.678353852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:37:12Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:37:12Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","conformance","crc","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-ewdrq","title":"add mounted-path ext4 getxattr/listxattr size-probe and ERANGE coverage","description":"Survey finding: ffs-fuse already implements Linux xattr size-probe and ERANGE behavior via classify_xattr_reply/reply_xattr_payload, but fuse_e2e only exercises successful payload reads. Scope: add ext4 mounted-path probes for getxattr and listxattr with size=0 length discovery plus undersized-buffer ERANGE on real FUSE paths; assert exact reported lengths, exact errno, and no state mutation. Acceptance: ext4 E2E proves size-probe and too-small-buffer behavior for both getxattr and listxattr.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:02:07.763102031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-ezmts","title":"[FUSE] fallocate FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE + FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE","description":"Implement the non-default fallocate(2) modes that ext4 supports natively, routed through ffs-fuse + ffs-extent.

Context:
- FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE | FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE: deallocate the [offset, offset+len) range, replacing it with a hole. File size unchanged. ext4 implements via ext4_punch_hole -> ext4_ext_remove_space.
- FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE: remove [offset, offset+len) and shift everything past it left by len. Length must be block-aligned. ext4 implements via ext4_collapse_range -> ext4_ext_shift_extents.
- FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE: inverse of collapse — shift right and insert a hole.
- FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE: convert range to unwritten extents (reads return zero, no physical alloc).

ffs-extent already has truncate_extents and split_extent helpers. Need a punch_extents that splits + frees the middle range without touching the tail; a shift_extents that subtracts a constant from logical_block of all extents past the cut; and the FUSE FUSE_FALLOCATE opcode dispatcher needs to fan out by mode flags.

Reference: fs/ext4/extents.c::ext4_ext_remove_space, ext4_ext_shift_extents.

Acceptance:
- Test: write 32 KiB, punch_hole(8K, 8K), read back -> first 8K data, next 8K zeros, next 16K data.
- Test: write 4 file-aligned chunks, collapse_range(4K, 4K) -> chunk2 disappears, chunks 3+4 shift down, file_size shrinks by 4K.

+{"id":"bd-oqphq","title":"[FUSE] statvfs filesystem-level metadata + free-space fragmentation","description":"Implement statvfs(3) / FUSE_STATFS so callers see accurate filesystem-level numbers — total/free/avail blocks + inodes — and a fragmentation-aware available count.

Context:
- POSIX statvfs requires: f_bsize, f_frsize, f_blocks, f_bfree, f_bavail, f_files, f_ffree, f_favail, f_fsid, f_flag, f_namemax. The kernel calls FUSE_STATFS (opcode 17), the FUSE driver translates the fuse_kstatfs reply into the kernel statfs.
- ext4 sources these from the superblock + group-descriptor cached counts (Ext4Superblock::s_free_blocks_count_lo + ext4 group desc bg_free_blocks_count) — these can drift from the actual bitmap state if a previous mount crashed before persisting.
- For accuracy under writeback the report must subtract pending uncommitted writes (MVCC version chains hold dirty blocks that look free in the bitmap but are actually in use).
- Free-space fragmentation: f_bavail traditionally = f_bfree minus reserved blocks. But for allocators that need contiguous regions (large extent writes), exposing the largest-contiguous-free-run via a side channel (e.g., FIEMAP on a synthetic free-space inode) helps callers like fallocate(FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE) plan ahead.

ffs-fuse already implements FsOps::statfs but the result is mostly hard-coded for tests; need to consult the actual ffs-alloc::GroupStats sums and subtract MVCC-pending writes.

Acceptance:
- Test: statvfs(mount_point).f_bavail decreases by N after writing N blocks.
- Test: after fallocate(KEEP_SIZE, big), f_bavail decreases by big-blocks (preallocated unwritten extents still consume space).
- A new ffs-core API: largest_contiguous_free_run() returns the size of the longest run of zero bits in the block bitmap, used by tests + future free-space-FIEMAP work.
- xfstests generic/{006, 027, 035} (statvfs accuracy) pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:29:37.163677090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:29:37.163677090Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-uzmsl","title":"[FUSE] name_to_handle_at + open_by_handle_at (NFS-style file handles)","description":"Implement encoding/decoding of opaque file handles so name_to_handle_at(2) and open_by_handle_at(2) work over the FUSE mount, matching ext4/btrfs's NFS-FH semantics.

Context:
- ext4 file handles are 8 bytes: 4-byte inode number + 4-byte NFS generation. FUSE_FILEHANDLE_LEN constraint is 128 bytes.
- btrfs handles encode subvolume objectid + inode objectid + generation.
- Linux fs/exportfs/expfs.c maps the kernel-level export_operations -> filesystem-specific encode_fh / decode_fh.
- For FUSE, the kernel does name_to_handle_at -> FUSE_LOOKUP -> uses inode's i_generation; open_by_handle_at -> FUSE_LOOKUP by handle (kernel-side translation, no FUSE op).

ffs-fuse currently exposes generation via to_file_attr but the FsOps trait has no  or  methods. Need to plumb a stable, generation-checked encode/decode that survives unmount/remount.

Acceptance:
- Test (in-process): encode_handle(ino) -> bytes; decode_handle(bytes) -> (ino, generation) match the on-disk inode.generation; if the inode is freed and reused, decode + verify -> ESTALE because generation mismatches.
- Test: a process opens a file via FUSE mount, calls name_to_handle_at, child process calls open_by_handle_at on the resulting handle, reads file contents successfully.
- xfstests generic/{426, 467, 477} (file handle round-trip) pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:28:07.500634913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:28:07.500634913Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-v1r","title":"Implement format-aware scrub validators for ext4/btrfs superblocks","description":"Scope:\n- Replace scrub CLI placeholder-only validator stack with format-aware validators.\n- Add ext4 superblock validator (parse + geometry + metadata checksum when enabled).\n- Add btrfs superblock validator (parse + superblock checksum).\n- Keep scrub engine format-agnostic with pluggable validators.\n- Add unit tests for both validators and validator selection in CLI path where practical.\n\nAcceptance:\n- cargo test for affected crates passes.\n- No regressions in existing scrub tests.\n- FEATURE_PARITY/ParityReport updated if capability counts change.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:20:52.577227565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:31:12.099229919Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:31:12.099209722Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4+btrfs superblock scrub validators, wired CLI composite validator stack, updated parity counts (self-healing 3/10, overall 29/75), and validated with cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace, plus targeted scrub/parity tests. cargo fmt --check still reports broad pre-existing formatting drift in unrelated files.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","repair"]}

 {"id":"bd-v62xw","title":"add ffs-core fuzz target for ext4 fast-commit evidence collection and verification","description":"Add a fuzz target around the ext4 fast-commit recovery path so malformed journal tails do not only rely on hand-written fixtures. Start from valid internal-journal seeds that advertise fast-commit blocks, then mutate the fast-commit tail bytes, transaction framing, and replay op payloads.

Targets:
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:3327-3377` (`maybe_collect_ext4_fast_commit_evidence`)
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:3380-3452` (`apply_fast_commit_operations` verification path)
- `ffs_journal::replay_fast_commit` through the mounted/open boundary

Acceptance:
- No panic on malformed fast-commit tails.
- Replay either returns structured parse errors or safe fallback-required evidence.
- Crash artifacts become regression tests or seeds under the shared fuzz corpus.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:31:42.013106273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:19.127823098Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:19.125492542Z","close_reason":"Covered by fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_openfs_journal_replay.rs (29KB umbrella harness): exercises OpenFs::open_with_options with Ext4JournalReplayMode, ExternalJournalInfo, Ext4FastCommitReplayEvidence, EXT4_ORPHAN_FS, EXT4_LAST_ORPHAN_OFFSET, JOURNAL_FAST_COMMIT_BLOCK, WalRecoveryReport — covers internal/external journal, fast-commit, orphan cleanup, and MVCC WAL replay through the real mount/open boundary as bd-p8c4q's acceptance requires.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fast-commit","ffs-core","fuzzing","recovery","testing"]}
